description: Backend Hardening Sprint — Extract service layer from fat controllers, add missing CRUD endpoints, implement input validation with consistent error responses, add role-based authorization, and introduce optimistic locking on key entities.

userStories.title: Typed exceptions and global exception filter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a global exception handling mechanism so all controllers return consistent error responses without repetitive try/catch blocks.
acceptanceCriteria: NotFoundException class created in Application/Exceptions/ — takes entity name and ID
ValidationException class created — takes Dictionary<string, List<string>> of field errors
ConflictException class created — takes message string
GlobalExceptionFilter created in API/Filters/ implementing IExceptionFilter
Filter catches NotFoundException → 404 with { error: message }
Filter catches ValidationException → 400 with { errors: { field: [messages] } }
Filter catches ConflictException → 409 with { error: message }
Filter catches UnauthorizedAccessException → 403 with { error: 'Acesso negado' }
Filter registered globally in Program.cs via AddControllers(o => o.Filters.Add<GlobalExceptionFilter>())
Unhandled exceptions still return 500 with generic message (no stack trace in response)
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Foundation for all other stories — every service will use these exceptions.

userStories.title: DI registration and service base pattern
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a consistent pattern for registering application services so adding new services is mechanical.
acceptanceCriteria: AddApplicationServices() extension method created on IServiceCollection in Application project
Method registers all service interfaces to implementations as Scoped
Called from Program.cs after AddInfrastructureServices()

At minimum, register placeholder interfaces and implementations for: ProductService, CategoryService, OrderService, PurchaseOrderService, CustomerService, SupplyService, DashboardService, FinanceService, InventoryService, UserService, FileService
Each service takes AppDbContext via constructor injection
Each interface file created with a single placeholder method (e.g., Task<T> GetByIdAsync(Guid id))
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Creates the scaffolding. Subsequent stories fill in the implementations.

userStories.title: Extract CategoryService from controller
userStories.description: As a developer, I want category business logic in a service so it is testable and controllers are thin HTTP adapters.
acceptanceCriteria: ICategoryService defines: GetByParentAsync, GetByIdAsync, GetTreeAsync, CreateAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync
CategoryService implements all methods, moving logic from CategoriesController
Parent-child hierarchy queries moved to service
Breadth-first tree traversal moved to service
Validation: Name required, Slug required+unique, ParentId must reference existing category, no circular refs
ValidationException thrown with all errors collected (not fail-on-first)
NotFoundException thrown when category not found
CategoriesController refactored to delegate to ICategoryService — no EF queries in controller
All existing category endpoints still work identically (same request/response shapes)
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Categories are simpler than Products — good first extraction to establish the pattern.

userStories.title: Extract ProductService from controller
userStories.description: As a developer, I want product business logic in a service so SKU generation, analytics, and variant management are testable.
acceptanceCriteria: IProductService defines: GetListAsync, GetByIdAsync, CreateAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync, GetVariantsAsync, CreateVariantAsync, UpdateVariantAsync, DeleteVariantAsync, GetAnalyticsAsync, GetRecentOrdersAsync
ProductService implements all methods, moving logic from ProductsController
SKU auto-generation logic moved to service
Category descendant recursion for filtering moved to service
Product analytics calculations moved to service
Profit distribution in recent orders moved to service
Cache key generation and invalidation logic moved to service (inject ICacheService)
Validation: Name required (max 200), SKU unique, Price >= 0, Cost >= 0, CategoryId must exist, Weight/dimensions >= 0
ProductsController refactored to thin adapter — no EF queries, no business logic
All existing product endpoints still work identically
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Largest extraction — includes SKU generation, analytics, cache, variants.

userStories.title: Extract OrderService from controller
userStories.description: As a developer, I want order business logic in a service so timeline building, cost management, and fulfillment are testable.
acceptanceCriteria: IOrderService defines: GetListAsync, GetByIdAsync, GetCostsAsync, AddCostAsync, UpdateCostAsync, DeleteCostAsync, FulfillAsync, CancelAsync
OrderService implements all methods, moving logic from OrdersController
Timeline building logic moved to service
Payment status derivation moved to service
Order cost CRUD moved to service with validation (value > 0, valid category)
Fulfillment with auto stock deduction moved to service
Cancellation logic moved to service
OrdersController refactored to thin adapter
All existing order endpoints still work identically
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Extract PurchaseOrderService from controller
userStories.description: As a developer, I want purchase order business logic in a service so cost allocation and status management are testable.
acceptanceCriteria: IPurchaseOrderService defines: GetListAsync, GetByIdAsync, CreateAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync, AddItemAsync, UpdateItemAsync, DeleteItemAsync, AddCostAsync, UpdateCostAsync, DeleteCostAsync, ReceiveAsync, CancelAsync
PurchaseOrderService implements all methods, moving logic from PurchaseOrdersController
Cost allocation logic (by_value, by_quantity distribution) moved to service
Status-based access controls (edit only if Rascunho) enforced in service
Validation: Supplier required, items must reference valid ProductId/VariantId, quantities > 0, unitCost >= 0
NEW: PUT /api/purchase-orders/{id}/costs/{costId} endpoint added via controller + service
PurchaseOrdersController refactored to thin adapter
All existing PO endpoints still work identically
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Includes the new PO cost update endpoint.

userStories.title: Extract DashboardService and FinanceService from controllers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want financial aggregation logic in services so dashboard and finance calculations are testable.
acceptanceCriteria: IDashboardService defines: GetSummaryAsync, GetRevenueChartAsync, GetCostBreakdownAsync, GetTopProductsAsync, GetLeastProfitableAsync, GetPendingActionsAsync
DashboardService implements all methods, moving logic from DashboardController
Top products ranking with profit distribution moved to service
Period parsing moved to service
IFinanceService defines: GetSummaryAsync, GetRevenueChartAsync, GetMarginChartAsync, GetSkuProfitabilityAsync, GetReconciliationAsync, GetAbcCurveAsync
FinanceService implements all methods, moving logic from FinanceController
SKU profitability aggregation moved to service
ABC curve classification moved to service
Monthly reconciliation logic moved to service
Both controllers refactored to thin adapters
All existing dashboard and finance endpoints still work identically
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Two services in one story since they share similar financial calculation patterns.

userStories.title: Extract remaining services (Customer, Supply, Inventory, File, Search, Notification)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all remaining controllers refactored to use services.
acceptanceCriteria: ICustomerService + CustomerService: GetListAsync, GetByIdAsync
ISupplyService + SupplyService: GetListAsync, GetByIdAsync (NEW), CreateAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync (NEW soft-delete)
IInventoryService + InventoryService: GetOverviewAsync, GetMovementsAsync, CreateMovementAsync
IFileService + FileService: UploadAsync, GetByEntityAsync, DeleteAsync
ISearchService + SearchService: SearchAsync
INotificationService + NotificationService: GetListAsync, MarkReadAsync, MarkAllReadAsync
NEW: GET /api/supplies/{id} endpoint returns full supply detail
NEW: DELETE /api/supplies/{id} sets IsActive=false (add IsActive to Supply entity if missing)
Supply validation: Name required (max 200), CurrentStock >= 0, MinimumStock >= 0, UnitCost >= 0
All 6 controllers refactored to thin adapters
All existing endpoints still work identically
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Batch extraction of simpler services. Includes new Supply endpoints.

userStories.title: User management service and endpoints
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to manage system users so I can create accounts, assign roles, and reset passwords.
acceptanceCriteria: IUserService defines: GetListAsync, GetByIdAsync, CreateAsync, UpdateAsync, DeactivateAsync, ResetPasswordAsync, ChangePasswordAsync
UserService implements all methods with bcrypt password hashing (BCrypt.Net-Next NuGet)
GET /api/settings/users/{id} returns user detail (no password hash)
POST /api/settings/users creates user — validates: Name required, Email required+valid+unique, Password min 8 chars, Role in [Admin,Manager,Viewer]
PUT /api/settings/users/{id} updates profile and role — validates same rules minus password
DELETE /api/settings/users/{id} deactivates user (soft-delete via IsActive flag)
POST /api/settings/users/{id}/reset-password — admin sets new password for user
POST /api/auth/change-password — self-service, requires current password verification
Password never returned in any DTO
SettingsController user endpoints delegate to IUserService
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: May need to add IsActive field to SystemUser entity. BCrypt.Net-Next is the recommended bcrypt library for .NET.

userStories.title: Role-based authorization on all endpoints
userStories.description: As an admin, I want role-based access control so viewers cannot modify data and only admins can manage settings.
acceptanceCriteria: JWT token generation includes role claim: new Claim(ClaimTypes.Role, user.Role)
AuthController login response includes user role
All Settings endpoints (users, integrations, costs) require Admin role
All POST/PUT/DELETE on Products, Categories, Supplies, POs require Manager or Admin role
All POST/PUT/DELETE on Orders (costs, fulfillment, cancel) require Manager or Admin role
All GET endpoints require any authenticated user (Viewer+)
Auth endpoints (login, refresh) remain anonymous
Change-password requires authenticated user (any role, self only)
Unauthorized role access returns 403 Forbidden
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Optimistic locking on key entities
userStories.description: As a user, I want to be notified when someone else has modified a record I am editing so I don't lose their changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Version property (int) added to Product, Category, PurchaseOrder, Supply entities
EF Core config: .Property(e => e.Version).IsConcurrencyToken() for all 4 entities
EF migration created adding version column (INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0) to 4 tables
All update DTOs for these entities include Version field (required)
Services check version match before saving; EF throws DbUpdateConcurrencyException on mismatch
DbUpdateConcurrencyException caught in services → throws ConflictException with Portuguese message
API returns 409 Conflict with message: 'Este registro foi modificado por outro usuário. Recarregue e tente novamente.'
All GET detail endpoints return Version in response DTO
Migration applies cleanly
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Run migration after adding Version properties. Existing rows get version=0.

userStories.title: Frontend 409 handling and version passthrough
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want the Angular app to handle optimistic locking conflicts gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: Error interceptor detects 409 status and shows specific toast: 'Este registro foi modificado por outro usuário. Recarregue a página.'
Product, Category, Supply, PurchaseOrder TypeScript interfaces include version: number field
All update service calls (product.service, category.service, supply.service, purchase-order.service) include version in PUT request body
After successful update, local state updated with new version from response
Angular build passes (ng build --configuration=development)
userStories.notes: Small frontend story — only changes needed for optimistic locking support.
